title: Cacalia mortonii
intro: Cacalia mortonii, also known as Morton's baccharis, is a flowering plant species of the Baccharis genus, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. Research has identified several health benefits associated with Cacalia mortonii, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as infections and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia mortonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cacalia mortonii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being infusions and decoctions used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cacalia mortonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Mexico. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Cacalia, species - C. mortonii. The classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. Further research may provide additional information on the species' classification.
